enonce.texte: Complete the sentences with the correct form of the verb in brackets: Present Perfect Simple or Past Simple.

questions.texte: She _______ (buy) a new car two days ago.

enonce.texte: Write questions using the Present Perfect with 'ever' and 'never'. Then answer them using the cues.

questions.reponse: Have you ever visited the United States?

sousQuestions.texte: Answer the question for yourself (use 'never' if you haven't, or give more information if you have).
sousQuestions.reponse: Example: No, I have never visited the United States. OR Yes, I have visited New York.

questions.reponse: Has she ever tried traditional African food?

sousQuestions.texte: Answer: She / try / fufu and ndolé (affirmative)
sousQuestions.reponse: Yes, she has tried fufu and ndolé.

questions.reponse: Have they ever been to the village?

sousQuestions.texte: Answer: They / be / there many times (affirmative)
sousQuestions.reponse: Yes, they have been there many times.

questions.reponse: Have you ever ridden a camel?

sousQuestions.texte: Answer: I / ride / one in northern Cameroon (affirmative)
sousQuestions.reponse: Yes, I have ridden one in northern Cameroon.

questions.texte: She has studied English _______ two years.
We haven't seen each other _______ last week.
He has worked at the hospital _______ ten months.
I have known him _______ a long time.

enonce.texte: Complete the sentences with the Present Perfect and 'just'.

intitule: Present Perfect vs Past Simple Explanation

enonce.texte: Identify whether the sentence uses Present Perfect or Past Simple, and explain why.

questions.texte: I have visited the Bimbia slave trade site three times.
questions.reponse: Present Perfect. Reason: It talks about an experience without specifying exactly when.

questions.texte: I visited the Bimbia slave trade site in 2019.
questions.reponse: Past Simple. Reason: The time (2019) is mentioned.

questions.texte: She has known him since primary school.
questions.reponse: Present Perfect. Reason: It talks about an action that started in the past and continues to the present ('since').

questions.texte: She knew him in primary school.
questions.reponse: Past Simple. Reason: It refers to a completed past action.

questions.texte: We haven't seen that movie yet.
questions.reponse: Present Perfect. Reason: It uses 'yet' (still waiting for the action) and is about a recent past action.

enonce.texte: Use the cues to write sentences about your life experiences, recent actions, and the duration of things. Use the Present Perfect Simple.

questions.texte: Write a paragraph (8-10 sentences) about your experiences. Include at least one experience, one recent action, one sentence with 'for', one with 'since', and one with 'just'.
questions.reponse:
Example answer:

I have lived in Cameroon my whole life. I have visited many regions, including the North and the Littoral. I have never been to the Far North region, but I would love to go one day. I have just returned from a trip to the Limbe Wildlife Centre. It was amazing! I have known my best friend since primary school. We have shared many memories together. I have also studied English for five years. I have already passed several exams.

sousQuestions.texte: Write about a person you know, using the Present Perfect (their experiences, duration, recent actions).
sousQuestions.reponse:
Example answer:

My mother has worked as a nurse for twenty years. She has helped many patients. She has just received an award for her service. She has worked at the same hospital since 2004. She has never considered changing her profession. She has also trained many young nurses. I have always admired her dedication.

enonce.texte: Find and correct the mistakes in the following sentences.

questions.texte: I have saw that movie already.
questions.reponse: I have seen that movie already.

questions.texte: She has gone to the market yesterday.
questions.reponse: She went to the market yesterday. (or She has gone to the market.)

questions.texte: They have been here since two hours.
questions.reponse: They have been here for two hours.

questions.texte: We have just finished our meal?
questions.reponse: We have just finished our meal. (remove the question mark if it's a statement)

enonce.texte: You are a journalist interviewing a famous Cameroonian athlete. Prepare six questions to ask them using the Present Perfect Simple and Past Simple appropriately.
images: A sports journalist holding a microphone interviewing an athlete in a stadium

questions.reponse:
Example questions:

1. Have you ever competed in an international competition?
2. When did you first start playing this sport?
3. Have you won any prizes?
4. Have you ever been injured during a match?
5. How long have you been a professional athlete?
6. Have you just returned from a competition?

enonce.texte: Choose the correct answer to complete each sentence.

questions.texte: We _______ (not / meet) each other for ten years.
They _______ (leave) the party at 10 pm last night.
